 {"id":"bd-1jdk","title":"Workstream: Model Selector + Scoped Cycling Parity","description":"# Goal
Match legacy model-selection UX:
- `Ctrl+L` opens a model selector.
- `Ctrl+P` / `Shift+Ctrl+P` cycles models forward/back within a scope.
- `/scoped-models` configures the scope for cycling.

# Legacy Spec
- Legacy README: Keyboard Shortcuts and Commands tables.
- Legacy docs:
  - `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/settings.md` (`enabledModels` patterns for cycling)

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- Interactive has `/model` command but no selector UI.
- Interactive has `/scoped-models` but:
  - no Ctrl+P cycling behavior
  - scope is not persisted
  - current Ctrl+P/N are used for input history navigation

# Scope / Deliverables
1) Model selector UI
- Show available models (from `ModelRegistry`) with fuzzy search.
- Selecting a model updates agent provider/model + persists to session header.

2) Scoped cycling
- Define the cycling list:
  - if `enabled_models`/`--models` patterns provided → use filtered list
  - else cycle all available models
- Implement forward/backward cycle with stable ordering.

3) `/scoped-models`
- Provide an in-app UI to edit scoped models (enable/disable patterns or pick from list).
- Persist scoped model patterns to settings (`enabled_models`).

# Dependencies
- Keybindings workstream (`bd-3ip`) is required to map Ctrl+L/Ctrl+P consistently.
- Unit tests for model registry scope logic are already tracked in `bd-2yd`.

# Testing
- State tests for model selector open/select.
- Deterministic tests for scoped cycling ordering.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Ctrl+L opens model selector
[ ] Ctrl+P cycles models forward; Shift+Ctrl+P cycles backward
[ ] /scoped-models configures cycling scope and persists
[ ] Cycling respects enabled_models patterns

 {"id":"bd-1kza","title":"Interactive: /share uploads session HTML as private GitHub gist","description":"# Goal
Implement gist-backed `/share` parity with legacy.

# Required Behavior
1) Verify `gh` exists.
2) Verify `gh auth status` succeeds.
3) Export session to a temp HTML file.

- If user cancels while `gh` is running:
  - kill the process
  - restore the editor
  - show status “Share cancelled”

 {"id":"bd-1mbs","title":"Editor: Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment","description":"# Goal
Support image attachment UX in interactive mode:
- Ctrl+V to paste an image from clipboard.
- Drag/drop a file onto the terminal to attach.

# Legacy Behavior
- On Ctrl+V, legacy tries to read clipboard image; if present, it writes to a temp file and inserts the file path at cursor.
  - See `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.ts` (`handleClipboardImagePaste`).

# Required Behavior
- Ctrl+V:
  - If clipboard contains an image, write it to a temp file (png/jpg based on mime if possible).
  - Insert the resulting path into the editor at the cursor.
  - If clipboard has no image or access denied, do nothing silently.

- Drag/drop:
  - Many terminals paste a quoted or escaped file path. Ensure we normalize/sanitize so that subsequent file reference processing can resolve it.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can attach an image without manually saving a file.
- [ ] No crashes when clipboard access is unavailable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:05.385818509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:42.357212214Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mh","title":"Update session picker to use SQLite index","description":"# Update session picker to use SQLite index

## Goal
Replace filesystem walking in session picker with fast SQLite index queries.

## Current Implementation (src/session_picker.rs)
Currently scans filesystem:
```rust
// Slow: walks all session directories
let sessions = walkdir::WalkDir::new(&sessions_dir)
    .into_iter()
    .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
    .filter(|e| e.path().extension() == Some("jsonl"))
    .collect();
```

## New Implementation
```rust
use crate::session_index::SessionIndex;

impl SessionPicker {
    pub fn load_sessions(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
        let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
        index.list_sessions(cwd)
    }
}
```

## UI Changes
- Display indexed metadata directly (no file reads needed)
- Show message count from index
- Show session name from index
- Faster render (no filesystem I/O)

## Fallback Behavior
If index unavailable or empty:
```rust
fn load_sessions_with_fallback(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
    // Try index first
    if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
        if let Ok(sessions) = index.list_sessions(cwd) {
            if !sessions.is_empty() {
                return Ok(sessions);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem (and populate index)
    let sessions = load_sessions_from_filesystem(cwd)?;
    // Optionally trigger reindex in background
    Ok(sessions)
}
```

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: Session picker shows indexed sessions
- Test: Picker falls back to filesystem if needed
- Test: Performance improved (measure time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker queries index by default
- [ ] Fallback to filesystem works
- [ ] UI displays index metadata
- [ ] Noticeably faster for 50+ sessions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.861448590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:20.352770513Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:20.352674283Z","close_reason":"Session picker uses index with fallback","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-3nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-24rp","title":"Docs: AGENTS.md - remove stale tokio migration statements","description":"## Goal
Update `AGENTS.md` where it describes tokio as still being retained/migrated, so contributor guidance matches the current repo.

## Background
`AGENTS.md` includes a “migration ongoing / tokio retained” narrative. In the current codebase, tokio/reqwest are no longer present, and `asupersync` is the async runtime.

## Scope
- Locate and update only the factual “current status” statements that are now incorrect.

 {"id":"bd-37z","title":"Spec: Hostcall ABI + capability manifest","description":"# Goal
Define a **precise hostcall ABI** and **capability manifest** so every extension I/O is explicit, auditable, and enforceable.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Request/response schema: `id`, `capability`, `method`, `params`, `timeout_ms`, `cancel_token`.
- Error taxonomy: deterministic mapping for timeouts, denied capability, IO failure.
- Streaming contract: chunked responses + backpressure signaling.
- Capability manifest schema: declarative list of required connectors + scope (paths, hosts, methods).
- Structured log schema: request_id, extension_id, capability, scope, duration, outcome, redaction flags.

# Security Invariants
- No hostcall executes without a granted capability.
- Capability scope is checked before any connector side effect.
- Logs are append-only and redact secrets by default.

# Tests This Enables
- ABI conformance fixtures (unit).
- Capability-deny tests with exact error mapping.
- E2E log assertions using the standardized schema.

# Non-goals
- Implicit global OS access.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:21:53.288452067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:06:18.183115804Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:04:41.049085160Z","close_reason":"Completed (spec+schema+Rust validation)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-37z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting bd-37z.  {"id":"bd-3am2","title":"Workstream: Error UX (actionable suggestions + context-aware hints)","description":"# Goal
Improve error UX so failures are not just "what happened" but also "what to do next" (actionable suggestions) and "why this happened here" (context-aware hints).

# Background
Pi runs in many environments (different terminals, missing system deps like `fd`, missing API keys, VCR playback mode, etc.). The port already has structured error types, but the user experience depends on:
- concise, readable rendering
- correct categorization
- a small set of high-signal remediation hints

# Principles
- Hints must be **actionable** (specific command/path/setting), not generic.
- Hints must be **non-spammy**: show at most a couple of suggestions per error.
- Hints must be **deterministic** and testable (unit/integration tests assert on messages).

# Candidate Hint Categories (examples)
- Missing API key → point to `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / etc and settings override.
- Missing system dependency (`fd`, `rg`) → exact install commands per OS.
- VCR cassette missing in CI → explain `VCR_MODE` + cassette dir + how to record.
- Permission denied reading/writing files → show the path and suggest chmod/ownership checks.
- Invalid config JSON → show file path + first parse error location.
- Provider auth failure (401) → suggest `/login` or `--api-key` usage.

# Deliverables
- A mapping from core error variants → short "next steps" hints.
- Rendering integration for:
  - interactive TUI error panels
  - print mode stderr
  - RPC mode error payloads (where appropriate)
- Tests validating the most important hint paths.

# Non-Goals
- Not building a full diagnostics engine.
- Not adding web browsing or auto-fixing.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:22:50.853156366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T21:22:50.858854034Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3am2","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3nix","title":"Workstream: /reload Parity (resources + autocomplete + diagnostics)","description":"# Goal
Bring `/reload` behavior to legacy parity.

# Legacy Behavior
In pi-mono (`interactive-mode.ts`), `/reload`:
- reloads the session resources
- applies themes
- rebuilds autocomplete
- (if extensions enabled) sets up extension shortcuts
- rebuilds chat from messages
- shows loaded resources summary
- surfaces models.json errors

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `/reload` calls `ResourceLoader::load(...)` and reports counts.
- It does not:
  - rebuild autocomplete
  - show models.json parse errors
  - reload context files (AGENTS)
  - reload extensions (beyond current extension workstream)

# Scope / Deliverables
1) After reload, update interactive state:
  - skills/prompts/themes collections
  - slash/template/skill command registry

 {"id":"bd-3nz","title":"Wire session indexing into session.persist()","description":"# Wire session indexing into session.persist()

## Goal
Automatically update the SQLite index when sessions are saved.

## Implementation

### Add Index Update to persist()
```rust
// src/session.rs

impl Session {
    pub async fn persist(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
        // Existing JSONL write logic
        self.write_jsonl(path).await?;
        
        // NEW: Update index
        if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
            if let Err(e) = index.index_session(path) {
                // Log warning but don't fail persist
                tracing::warn!("Failed to update session index: {}", e);
            }
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Default Index Path
```rust
// src/session_index.rs

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open_default() -> Result<Self> {
        let path = dirs::data_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("."))
            .join(".pi/agent/sessions/index.db");
        Self::open(&path)
    }
}
```

### Handle Index Creation
- Create index.db if not exists
- Use WAL mode for concurrent access
- Handle permission errors gracefully

## Considerations
1. **Performance**: Index update should be fast (<10ms)
2. **Reliability**: Don't fail persist if index fails
3. **Concurrency**: Handle multiple pi instances
4. **Migration**: Handle existing sessions without index entries

## Testing
- Test: persist() updates index
- Test: persist() succeeds even if index fails
- Test: Index contains session after persist

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] persist() calls index_session()
- [ ] Index created if not exists
- [ ] Errors logged but don't fail persist

+# Troubleshooting
+
+This page collects common failures and practical fixes. If a behavior is still
+being implemented, the relevant bead ID is listed for tracking.
+
+## API keys and auth
+
+**Symptom:** `Missing API key` or provider auth errors.
+
+**Fixes:**
+- Use env vars: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, etc.
+- Or set `--api-key` per run.
+- Or store credentials in `~/.pi/agent/auth.json` via `/login` (Anthropic OAuth).
+
+**Config precedence (most → least):**
+1. `--api-key`
+2. Provider-specific env var
+3. `auth.json` (OAuth or stored API key)
+
+## Provider errors (401/429/5xx)
+
+**401/403:** Key missing or invalid. Confirm correct provider and key.
+
+**429:** Rate limit. Pi will retry if `retry.enabled` is true in settings.
+
+**5xx/network:** Temporary provider outage or flaky network. Retry or switch models.
+
+**Retry config** lives in `~/.pi/agent/settings.json`:
+```json
+{
+  "retry": {
+    "enabled": true,
+    "maxRetries": 3,
+    "baseDelayMs": 1000,
+    "maxDelayMs": 30000
+  }
+}
+```
+
+## VCR mode (tests)
+
+Tests that exercise provider streaming use recorded cassettes for determinism.
+
+Environment variables:
+- `VCR_MODE=record|playback|auto`
+- `VCR_CASSETTE_DIR=tests/fixtures/vcr` (default)
+
+Common fixes:
+- Missing cassette: run with `VCR_MODE=record` once, then commit the cassette.
+- No network in CI: use `VCR_MODE=playback`.
+- Invalid `VCR_MODE`: only `record`, `playback`, or `auto` are accepted.
+
+## Packages and extensions
+
+**Symptom:** extensions or skills not found.
+
+**Fixes:**
+- Check package sources via `pi list`.
+- Confirm settings in `~/.pi/agent/settings.json` or `.pi/settings.json`.
+- Re-run `pi update` after adding a source.
+
+Extension discovery is tracked under **bd-1e0** (install + resolution). If an
+extension fails to load, expect diagnostics to improve as that bead lands.
+
+## Sessions (persistence + recovery)
+
+Sessions live under:
+```
+~/.pi/agent/sessions/
+```
+
+Overrides:
+- `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR` (global base)
+- `PI_SESSIONS_DIR` (sessions root)
+
+**Corruption recovery:**
+- Run with `--no-session` to bypass persistence.
+- Move the offending `.jsonl` file out of the sessions dir.
+
+Interactive UX parity for `/resume`, `/tree`, `/fork` is tracked by **bd-14cc**.
+
+## Keybindings & hotkeys
+
+Keybindings are loaded from:
+```
+~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json
+```
+
+If shortcuts don’t work as expected:
+- Delete/rename the file to fall back to defaults.
+- Confirm your terminal isn’t intercepting the keys.
+
+Full keybinding parity (including `/hotkeys`) is tracked by **bd-3ip**.
+
+## Terminal quirks
+
+Some terminals reserve key combos (especially on Windows):
+- `Ctrl+Enter` / `Alt+Enter` may be intercepted.
+- Paste events can differ between terminals.
+
+If a shortcut doesn’t trigger, try a different terminal or remap the key.
+Interactive editor parity (autocomplete/bang/paste) is tracked by **bd-1iwi**.
+
+## Missing system dependencies
+
+The `find` tool requires `fd`:
+```bash
+# Ubuntu/Debian
+apt install fd-find
+
+# macOS
+brew install fd
+
+# The binary might be named fdfind
+ln -s $(which fdfind) ~/.local/bin/fd
+```
+
+`rg` (ripgrep) is optional but recommended for faster searches.
+
+## Tool output truncated
+
+Large tool outputs are truncated to protect the context window. Ask for
+specific ranges (e.g., “Read lines 2000-4000 of that file”).
